name: Peacock
summary: The peacock is one of the most famous and beautiful birds in the world. Known for the male's spectacular train of iridescent blue-green tail feathers covered in colorful eyespots, they use their stunning display to attract peahens and scare off predators.
metaTitle: Peacock Facts | Habitat, Diet, Feathers & Photos
metaDescription: Peacock facts for kids with photos, tropical habitat, diet, behavior, and tail feather displays on one easy-to-read page.
adaptations: sharp spurs on legs for defense
funFacts: Only the males are called peacocks; females are called peahens, and babies are called peachicks. Together they are called peafowl!
A male peacock's train contains over 150 feathers, each decorated with beautiful eye-like spots called ocelli.
To attract a female, the peacock shakes his giant fan of feathers, which makes a rustling sound like wind through leaves.
Despite their huge tails, peacocks are capable of flight—they fly up into tall trees to sleep at night to stay safe from predators.
Peacocks are famous in India for eating small snakes, even venomous young cobras, making them welcome protectors in villages.
Their tail feathers shed naturally every year after the breeding season, so no birds are harmed to collect their feathers.

faq.question: What is a peacock?
faq.answer: A peacock is a large, colorful bird belonging to the pheasant family. They are famous for their giant tails and bright blue heads and necks.

faq.question: Why do peacocks display their feathers?
faq.answer: Male peacocks spread their tail feathers into a large fan to impress female peahens during the breeding season. The peahen chooses a mate based on the size and beauty of his display.

faq.question: Can peacocks fly?
faq.answer: Yes, peacocks can fly! Although they spend most of their time walking on the ground, they can fly short distances to escape danger or roost in tall branches at night.
